name: Salvaterra do Extremo from Zarza la Mayor — circular by the Río Erjas
name_original: Salvaterra do Extremo desde Zarza la Mayor — circular por el río Erjas
region_intro: Hiking around Zarza La Mayor offers a diverse landscape for outdoor exploration. The region features a mix of riverine environments along the Río Erjas, historical livestock trails, and pathways leading to ancient landmarks. Elevation changes are generally gentle, with some routes featuring moderate ascents. The terrain includes open countryside and paths that follow traditional routes. Best hiking trails around Zarza La Mayor The most popular hiking route is Peñafiel Castle Trail, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This route features historical points of interest and gentle ascents. Another top favourite among local hikers is Menhir del Cabezo from Piedras Albas — circular via Ruta de la Cañada Real de Gata, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail winds through historical pathways and offers views of the surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Salvaterra do Extremo from Zarza la Mayor — circular by the Río Erjas, a 14.1 miles (22.6 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes along the Río Erjas, often completed in about 6 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Zarza La Mayor is defined by river valleys, historical trails, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Zarza La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Zarza La Mayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Zarza La Mayor?
A: Zarza La Mayor offers a network of over 10 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes explore the region's diverse landscapes, including riverine environments and historical pathways.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Zarza La Mayor?
A: Yes, Zarza La Mayor has several easy hiking options. Approximately 20% of the trails are classified as easy, providing gentle terrain and shorter distances perfect for a relaxed walk. These routes often follow traditional paths through open countryside.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance hikes in Zarza La Mayor?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Zarza La Mayor features at least one difficult route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304481798/salvaterra-do-extremo-from-zarza-la-mayor-circular-by-the-rio-erjas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvaterra do Extremo from Zarza la Mayor — circular by the Río Erjas&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, covering over 22 km and typically taking more than 6 hours to complete, with significant elevation changes along the Río Erjas.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Zarza La Mayor?
A: Hikes in Zarza La Mayor vary in duration. Shorter, moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304363784/penafiel-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñafiel Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 2.5 hours, while longer routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304481798/salvaterra-do-extremo-from-zarza-la-mayor-circular-by-the-rio-erjas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvaterra do Extremo from Zarza la Mayor — circular by the Río Erjas&lt;/a&gt; can take over 6 hours.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Zarza La Mayor?
A: Many of the trails in Zarza La Mayor, particularly the easier and moderate ones with gentle ascents and historical points of interest, are suitable for families. These routes offer a chance to explore the countryside without overly strenuous sections.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Zarza La Mayor?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Zarza La Mayor, especially in open countryside areas. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, particularly near livestock or in protected natural areas, and to carry water for them.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Zarza La Mayor?
A: Along the trails, you can explore historical points of interest and natural features. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6110369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñafiel Castle of Zarza la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5331083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Buraca (Canchal de los Ojos) and Medieval Tombs&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6110848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura International Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also follow the scenic Río Erjas.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Zarza La Mayor?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available, offering convenient starting and ending points. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304682131/menhir-del-cabezo-from-piedras-albas-circular-via-ruta-de-la-canada-real-de-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menhir del Cabezo from Piedras Albas — circular via Ruta de la Cañada Real de Gata&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to experience varied landscapes without retracing your steps.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Zarza La Mayor?
A: The best time for hiking in Zarza La Mayor is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant, with comfortable temperatures for outdoor activities.

Q: Is hiking possible in Zarza La Mayor during winter?
A: Hiking is generally possible in Zarza La Mayor during winter, as the region experiences relatively mild winters compared to other parts of Europe. However, it's important to check local weather forecasts for rain and cooler temperatures, and dress in layers.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while hiking in Zarza La Mayor?
A: The diverse landscapes of Zarza La Mayor, especially along the Río Erjas and in the open countryside, provide opportunities for wildlife spotting. Keep an eye out for various bird species and other local fauna, particularly during quieter times of the day.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Zarza La Mayor?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Zarza La Mayor, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 7 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's mix of riverine environments, historical trails, and the peaceful open countryside.
tips: Zarza la Mayor is a Spanish town and municipality, located in the province of Cáceres, in the autonomous community of Extremadura. It belongs to the judicial district of Cáceres and is the westernmost town in the community of Rivera de Fresnedosa. Furthermore, it is the only municipality in said community that has a border with Portugal, located four kilometers in a straight line from Salvaterra do Extremo.
The Cañada Real de Gata is a short-distance hiking route that offers great scenic value as it passes entirely through the surroundings of the Tajo Internacional Natural Park in Extremadura.

The Erjas River (also known as the Eljas River in Spain and the Erges River in Portugal) is a tributary of the Tagus River that serves as a natural border between Spain and Portugal for approximately 50 km.
Salvaterra do Extremo is a picturesque village in Portugal, known for its stunning views and natural surroundings.
The Mirador de la Caseta del Río Erjas, also known as the Eljas River in Portuguese, is a picturesque place that offers stunning views on the border between Spain and Portugal.
